zh.lifeSummary: 當Adelise de Peverel於大約1075年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾時，她的父親Sir William de Peverel27歲，母親Adeline16歲。她和Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers至少有6個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1156年5月27日之後在英國英格蘭哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特，埋葬在英國哈普爾易斯爾 本 懷特德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Peverel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Peverall
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Old French Peurel (Latin Piperellus), a diminutive of Old French pevre ‘pepper’ (Anglo-Norman French peivere, Latin piper), originally perhaps used as a nickname on the Continent but used as a personal name in Anglo-Norman England.

nameMeanings.name: Adelise
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: diminutive of Adèle ( see Adele ), used occasionally in England from the early 16th century onwards, and now also found in the wider English-speaking world.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Adelise de Peverel was born about 1075, in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Sir William de Peverel, was 27 and her mother, Adeline, was 16. She had at least 6 sons and 3 daughters with Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers. She died after 27 May 1156, in Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Ryde, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adelise de Peverel est née environ 1075, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William de Peverel, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Adeline, avait 16 ans. Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles avec Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers. Elle est décédée après 27 mai 1156, à Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Ryde, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adelise de Peverel um 1075 in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Peverel 27 und ihre Mutter Adeline 16. Sie hatte mit Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb nach 27. Mai 1156 in Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Ryde, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adelise de Peverel è nata il circa 1075 nel Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Sir William de Peverel, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Adeline, aveva 16 anni. Lei avuto almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie con Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers. Morì dopo il 27 maggio 1156, nel Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a Ryde, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Adelise de Peverelが約1075年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir William de Peverelは27歳，母Adelineは16歳であった。彼女はRichard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviersとの間に少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1156年5月27日以後にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアイスル オフ ウィトで死去し，イギリスハンプシュアイアイスル オフ ウィトリドにて埋葬された。

ko.Ryde, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England: 영국 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트 리드
ko.Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트
ko.lifeSummary: Adelise de Peverel(이)가 대략 1075년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Peverel(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adeline(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1156년 5월 27일 이후 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트에서 사망했으며 영국 함프실르 이슬일 프 와이트 리드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adelise de Peverel nasceu aproximadamente 1075, em Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir William de Peverel, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Adeline, tinha 16 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas com Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers. Ela faleceu após 27 de maio de 1156, em Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Ryde, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Ryde, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England: раид, исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия
ru.Isle of Wight, Hampshire, England: исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adelise de Peverel родилась около 1075 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Sir William de Peverel было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Adeline, 16. У неё было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери от Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers. Она умерла после 27 май 1156 г. в местности исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности раид, исл оф ваит, хампшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adelise de Peverel nació aproximadamente 1075, en Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir William de Peverel, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Adeline, tenía 16 años. Tuvo por lo menos 6 hijos y 3 hijas con Richard de Vernon Seigneur de Reviers. Murió después de 27 de mayo de 1156, en Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Ryde, Isle of Wight, Hampshire, Inglaterra.
